findings.recommendation: Ensure 'cell_size' is calculated (e.g., from terrain width and resolution) or extracted from 'params' before calling 'compute_world_splatmap_weights'. This appears to be a NameError regression in both 'handle_generate_terrain_tile' and 'handle_generate_world_terrain'.
findings.title: Undefined Variable 'cell_size' in Splatmap Generation

consensus_findings.evidence: if params.get("tiled_world") or params.get("use_global_height_range"): return _resolve_height_range(params, heightmap)

findings.evidence: if params.get("tiled_world") or params.get("use_global_height_range"): return _resolve_height_range(params, heightmap)
findings.recommendation: For tiled worlds, heightmaps must be normalized against a stable global vertical range to ensure height continuity at tile edges. '_resolve_height_range' currently falls back to the local min/max of the input 'heightmap', which will cause tiles with different elevation profiles to be stretched inconsistently when exported to 16-bit RAW.
findings.title: Height Normalization Breakage in Tiled World Export

consensus_findings.recommendation: For tiled worlds, heightmaps must be normalized against a stable global vertical range to ensure height continuity at tile edges. '_resolve_height_range' currently falls back to the local min/max of the input 'heightmap', which will cause tiles with different elevation profiles to be stretched inconsistently when exported to 16-bit RAW.
consensus_findings.title: Height Normalization Breakage in Tiled World Export

findings.recommendation: Reshaping flattened vertex data assumes Blender maintains a strict grid-aligned vertex order. This is brittle and will fail if the mesh has been sculpted, optimized, or had its topology modified. Use a ray-casting or bmesh-based grid sampler to extract height values from the mesh bounds safely.
findings.title: Brittle Grid Reshaping for Sculpted Terrain Meshes

consensus_findings.key: tools mcp toolkit blender addon handlers environment py handle export heightmap brittle grid reshaping for sculpted
consensus_findings.recommendation: Reshaping flattened vertex data assumes Blender maintains a strict grid-aligned vertex order. consensus_findings.title: Brittle Grid Reshaping for Sculpted Terrain Meshes

findings.recommendation: Ensure 'half' is explicitly defined in this scope. While it may be defined in the parent function, the reliance on center-origin calculations here without a local definition of 'half' (presumably terrain_size / 2) is risky and inconsistent with the previous corner-origin logic.
findings.title: Inconsistent Origin Handling in World Anchor Planning
